prompt: The remove removes by index instead of value, causing a wrong element to be deleted. What is the bug?
choices: List.remove() always removes by value for Integer lists
list.remove(1) calls remove(int index) — it removes the element at index 1 (value 20), not the element with value 1
remove() is not available on ArrayList
Integer autoboxing means remove(1) always removes value 1
explanation: List has two remove overloads: remove(int index) and remove(Object o). Passing the primitive int 1 calls remove(int index), deleting index 1 (value 20), not value 1. To remove by value, use remove(Integer.valueOf(1)) or cast: remove((Integer)1).
